name: Ginger-Almond Florentines
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Firmly packed dark brown sugar
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Williams-Sonoma Kitchen Library - Cookies & Breads
Preparation Time: varies
Prepare the chocolate coating first. Line 2 large, heavy baking sheets with aluminum foil. Lightly butter the foil.
In a heavy saucepan, combine cream, granulated sugar, dark brown sugar, and butter. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, just until the sugars dissolve and the butter melts. Add sliced almonds, all-purpose flour, crystallized ginger, orange zest, and lemon zest. Bring the mixture to a boil, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat and drop 1 tablespoon of batter onto prepared baking sheets, spacing cookies about 3 inches apart. Repeat with the second sheet.
Bake until deep brown, about 10 minutes. Remove from oven. Using a round cookie cutter or a glass that is 3 inches in diameter, push hot cookie edges inward to neaten them and shape into 3-inch rounds. Slide foil off sheets and line the same sheets with new foil, buttering it again.
Repeat with remaining batter. Cool cookies completely on foil. Carefully peel cookies off foil and arrange them, smooth-side up, on baking sheets. Spoon 1 teaspoon of hot chocolate coating into the center of each cookie and spread to edges using a small icing spatula or knife. Refrigerate until chocolate is set, about 30 minutes.
Store layered between waxed paper in an airtight container in refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. Makes about 2 dozen cookies.
